response:
Governance Automation (Wisdm) — RFP Section

Objective
- Provide an automated governance solution that enforces policy-as-code across the Wisdm Azure estate, continuously detects and remediates configuration drift, and produces auditor-ready compliance evidence and reports mapped to SOC2, ISO 27001 and NIST CSF. Solution must be delivered as automated, repeatable, and auditable components suitable for enterprise-scale operations.

Scope
- Azure resources owned or managed under Wisdm subscriptions.
- Policy enforcement and remediation for resource configuration, naming/tagging, allowed regions, SKU restrictions, networking and security guardrails.
- Continuous drift detection and automated remediation for resource assignments and telemetry artifacts (e.g., Data Collection Rules).
- Compliance evidence collection, mapping to SOC2/ISO27001/NIST CSF controls, and automated report generation for auditors.
- Integration with Wisdm CMDB/source-of-truth and existing identity/role model (RBAC, service principals).

Mandatory Requirements
1. Policy-as-code enforcement
   - Use Azure Policy / initiative constructs for all enforceable controls.
   - Provide a reusable policy catalog including at minimum: naming/tagging enforcement, allowed regions, SKU/size restrictions, Key Vault & secret rotation baseline, NSG baseline rules, AKS and App Service guardrails.
   - All policies must be implemented as code (ARM/Bicep/Terraform + Azure Policy definitions) and stored in Wisdm’s IaC repo with CI/CD.

2. Compliance mapping & reporting
   - Map policy definitions and controls to SOC2, ISO 27001 and NIST CSF requirements.
   - Produce automated compliance reports that include evidence items, control status, remediation history and remediation proof.
   - Reduce manual auditor preparation effort from ~2 weeks to ≤ 4 hours per audit via automated evidence export (PDF/CSV) and control traceability.

3. Real-time and scheduled drift detection
   - Implement continuous drift detection for resource configuration and critical telemetry artifacts (e.g., Data Collection Rules, DCR assignments).
   - Reconciliation loop comparing desired state (CMDB) against actual Azure state (Azure Resource Graph/ARM).
   - Daily full-scan drift detection with real-time alerts for critical drift events.

4. Automated remediation
   - Provide automated remediation for common drift scenarios via Azure Policy remediation tasks, Azure Automation/Logic Apps/Azure Functions and runbooks.
   - Target automated remediation coverage for ≥ 80% of common drift scenarios; remaining scenarios must have automated remediation playbooks that trigger human approval workflows.
   - Log remediation attempts and results for audit.

5. Evidence & audit trail
   - Centralized, immutable evidence repository for compliance artifacts, control evidence, remediation proofs and change logs.
   - Ensure database audit trails and change provenance recorded for configuration and control changes (integrate existing DB audit mechanisms where applicable).

6. Operational dashboards & alerts
   - Role-based dashboards (operations, security, compliance, auditors) using Azure Monitor Workbooks.
   - Scheduled report export (PDF) and API access to compliance summaries for downstream tools.
   - Alerting for policy violations, failed remediations, and drift events to designated on-call teams.

Technical Approach (high-level layered architecture)
- Layer 1: Policy Definitions
  - Author and publish Azure Policy definitions (built from policy-as-code repository) enforcing desired resource configurations.
  - Enforce naming and tagging conventions and rax- prefix convention for managed artifacts (e.g., rax-* for Wisdm-managed DCRs).

- Layer 2: Policy Initiatives and Control Mapping
  - Group policies into initiatives aligned to compliance frameworks (SOC2 control sets, ISO 27001 annex controls, NIST CSF categories).
  - Maintain mapping documentation that links each initiative/policy to specific framework controls for traceability.

- Layer 3: Automated Remediation & Reconciliation
  - Implement automatic remediation using Azure Policy’s remediation tasks where safe; otherwise use runbooks (Azure Automation), Logic Apps or Functions with approval gates.
  - Implement a reconciliation loop that compares CMDB desired state against Azure Resource Graph and resource properties (including DCR assignments). Run daily scans and reconcile after scale/migration events.

- Layer 4: Evidence Collection & Reporting
  - Use Azure Monitor Workbooks for interactive dashboards and scheduled PDF/CSV export for auditors.
  - Collect and store evidentiary artifacts in an immutable store (versioned blobs with access controls).
  - Integrate evidence collection agents or telemetry (e.g., DCRs for monitoring) and ensure monitoring assignments are validated post-scale events.

Drift Detection Details (must-haves)
- Daily full reconciliation scan: compare CMDB desired mapping to actual Azure assignments using Azure Resource Graph and API queries.
- Real-time detection for high-risk changes using Event Grid + Azure Functions to evaluate against policy and desired-state rules.
- Enforce naming conventions (rax- prefix) to differentiate Wisdm-managed artifacts and exclude customer-created exceptions.
- OS-aware DCR assignment validation: verify Windows vs Linux DCR mapping and remediate mismatches automatically.
- Report drift metrics and remediation success/failure in dashboards.

Acceptance Criteria & Key Metrics
- Policy compliance rate: ≥ 95% of applicable policy controls in compliant state within first 30 days of onboarding (measured by automated policy scan).
- Automated remediation coverage: ≥ 80% of defined common drift scenarios automatically remediated.
- Compliance report generation time: auditor-ready evidence pack generated within ≤ 4 hours (versus baseline ~2 weeks).
- Drift detection cadence: daily reconciliation scans; critical-drift alerting within 15 minutes of detection.
- Remediation SLA: automated remediations complete within configurable window (default ≤ 2 hours); human-approved remediations have documented approval workflows with 24-hour SLAs.

Deliverables
- Policy-as-code repository (definitions + initiatives) and CI/CD pipeline.
- Control mapping documentation (SOC2, ISO 27001, NIST CSF).
- Automated remediation playbooks, runbooks and Logic Apps/Functions.
- Drift detection and reconciliation service implementation and runbook.
- Dashboards (roles-based) and scheduled report templates with sample PDF/CSV exports.
- Evidence repository and retention/configuration documentation.
- Operational runbook, training for Wisdm teams, and handover package.

Implementation Phases (recommended)
1. Discovery & baseline: inventory, CMDB alignment, control prioritization.
2. Policy codification & pilot: implement core policies and pilot in a staging subscription.
3. Automation & remediation: add remediation playbooks and reconciliation loop.
4. Reporting & evidence: build dashboards and automated report exports.
5. Pilot validation & rollout: onboard first business units and measure against KPIs.
6. Handover & runbook: operationalize and transfer to Wisdm operations.

Assumptions
- Wisdm will provide CMDB access and agreed desired-state mappings.
- Azure subscriptions will permit Azure Policy initiatives, Azure Monitor and necessary service principals.
- Owner-specified exceptions will be captured and handled via documented approval workflows.

If you want, we can expand this into a formal RFP text block with insertable sections (requirements matrix, evaluation criteria, scoring rubric and example policy catalog) for direct inclusion in Wisdm’s RFP document.
context_injected:
--- Episode 1 [manual 2026-03-17] (similarity: 0.729) ---
Wisdm RFP preparation — governance automation section. Wisdm requires automated governance capabilities including: policy-as-code enforcement via Azure Policy, automated compliance reporting (SOC2, ISO 27001, NIST CSF), and real-time drift detection on resource configurations. Key metrics to include: 95% policy compliance rate within first 30 days of onboarding, automated remediation for 80% of common drift scenarios, compliance report generation reduced from 2 weeks manual effort to 4 hours automated.

--- Episode 2 [claude-code 2026-03-17] (similarity: 0.5186) ---
Wisdm governance automation technical approach: Layer 1 — Azure Policy definitions for resource compliance (naming, tagging, allowed regions, SKU restrictions). Layer 2 — Custom policy initiatives grouped by compliance framework (SOC2 controls mapped to Azure Policy). Layer 3 — Automated remediation tasks triggered by policy violations. Layer 4 — Dashboard and reporting via Azure Monitor Workbooks with scheduled PDF export for auditors.

--- Episode 3 [manual 2026-03-17] (similarity: 0.3549) ---
Project Sentinel customer pipeline update: Contoso Financial has signed the pilot agreement — starting with SOC2 compliance automation. Woodgrove Bank is in legal review. Fabrikam Healthcare wants HIPAA focus first, then SOC2. Adatum Insurance expressed interest but needs budget approval in Q2. Northwind Traders declined — they went with a competing solution from ServiceNow.

--- Episode 4 [manual 2026-03-17] (similarity: 0.3382) ---
SOX compliance review for AI development practices at Rackspace. Key outcomes: (1) All AI model deployments must go through the existing change management process with CAB approval. (2) Database audit trails must capture who changed what and when — pgAudit handles this for PostgreSQL workloads. (3) AI training data must have provenance tracking — Borg's episode source_episodes array satisfies this requirement. (4) Approval gates are required at three stages: code review, staging validation, and production deployment.

--- Episode 5 [claude-code 2026-03-17] (similarity: 0.3181) ---
Designing the AMA v3 DCR drift detection system. Key considerations: all Data Collection Rules follow the rax- prefix naming convention (e.g., rax-windows-perf, rax-linux-syslog). DCR assignment depends on OS categorization — Windows servers get different DCRs than Linux. The v2 schema had drift issues because DCR assignments weren't validated after scale events. v3 needs a reconciliation loop that compares desired state (from CMDB) against actual DCR assignments in Azure Monitor.

--- Episode 6 [claude-code 2026-03-17] (similarity: 0.3104) ---
Decision: Use pgAudit for SOX compliance on all Borg database tables. Rationale: pgAudit is natively supported on Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server, requires no additional licensing, and generates audit logs that satisfy SOX Section 404 requirements for change tracking and access control evidence. All production changes to AI systems require approval gates — no direct commits to main branch without PR review.

--- Episode 7 [manual 2026-03-17] (similarity: 0.2978) ---
AMA compliance tracking v2 post-mortem: the main drift risk was that DCR assignments could silently change during Azure maintenance or subscription migrations. The rax- prefix convention helps identify Rackspace-managed DCRs vs customer-created ones. OS categorization (Windows/Linux/Other) determines which DCR set gets applied. v3 should add a daily drift scan comparing ARM resource graph state against the expected DCR mapping table.

--- Episode 8 [claude-code 2026-03-17] (similarity: 0.2973) ---
Project Sentinel architecture review. Sentinel is a multi-agent AI compliance automation system using 6 specialized agents built on Semantic Kernel and Azure AI Foundry. The agents handle: (1) policy ingestion, (2) control mapping, (3) evidence collection, (4) gap analysis, (5) remediation recommendations, (6) report generation. Currently targeting Contoso Financial, Woodgrove Bank, and Fabrikam Healthcare as pilot customers. Discussions also included Adatum Insurance and Northwind Traders for phase 2.

--- Episode 9 [manual 2026-03-17] (similarity: 0.2811) ---
Azure-PROD entity changes this month: New service principal created for Sentinel (sp-sentinel-prod) with contributor role scoped to rax-prod-sentinel-rg. AeroPro AKS cluster upgraded from 1.28 to 1.29. Front Door WAF policy updated to include new OWASP 3.2 ruleset. Key Vault rotation policy changed from 90-day to 60-day rotation cycle for all managed certificates. These changes supersede the previous 90-day rotation convention documented in February.

--- Episode 10 [manual 2026-03-17] (similarity: 0.2749) ---
Azure-PROD schema change log — March 2026: (1) Added new resource group rax-prod-sentinel-rg for Project Sentinel pilot. (2) Updated NSG rules on rax-prod-apim-subnet to allow outbound to Azure AI Foundry endpoints. (3) Migrated 3 App Service plans to AKS node pools (AeroPro workloads). (4) Added Azure Monitor DCR rax-prod-sentinel-dcr for Sentinel agent telemetry. (5) Deprecated the legacy rax-prod-monitoring-rg resource group — resources moved to rax-prod-observability-rg.
